topic: Gyroscopic precession rate 4.87e-12 rad/s² — duplicated text bug + standing units/value/post-hoc critique
change_summary: detail_evidence grew from 1085 to 1900 chars (+75%) since c2 (2026-04-07). detail_verdict_text grew 189→1074 chars (added the structural post-hoc paragraph + IAU/post-hoc cross-reference). Investigation reveals the evidence growth is entirely a duplicated-sentences bug, not new content.
our_argument_summary: Three-failure argument unchanged from c2: (1) units error (rad/s² is angular acceleration, not the angular velocity required for a precession rate); (2) ~37% numerical mismatch even with charitable unit correction (4.87e-12 vs IAU 2006 value 7.7257e-12 rad/s); (3) no derivation from dome geometry, plus monitor.py omission. The detail_verdict_text now also adds the Part 3.1b structural critique (post-hoc transcription of textbook value, no legitimate-novelty carve-out, hardcoded expected sits in same commit as fetch call). Verdict 'Misleading' still appropriate.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (line 106 — static WIN-021 entry, no code path)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. The static WIN-021 entry asserts value 4.87e-12 rad/s² with no script that derives or validates it. raw-text/02-wins.txt confirms this is presented as a confirmed result with no fetch or computation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static assertion. No pred==obs comparison in any code path. Tagged 'monitoring: none' which is correct.
data_sources_used: None — no external data fetched for this WIN
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our text says 'monitor.py does not include gyroscopic precession in its multi-domain audit engine' (post-c2 minor edit) — accurate. The earlier 'in its 39-domain audit engine' phrasing is also still in the text (see HOLE-001 below) — that older phrase is now stale (raw-text snapshot suggests audit engine domain count has drifted) but not factually wrong against the snapshot.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 106 is a static WIN-021 entry. No fetch, no comparison, no domain in any audit engine for this claim. The c2 review already corrected this tag from 'hardcoded' to 'none' and the wins.json entry now reflects 'none'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics value (50.2881 arcsec/yr → 7.7257e-12 rad/s, IAU 2006 / Capitaine et al. 2003) does NOT match the dome's 4.87e-12 rad/s² claim — 37% off after charitable unit conversion. This is a failed independent prediction, not a relabeling.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Earth's axial precession has been measured since Hipparchus (~130 BC); the IAU 2006 value is decades old. The dome adopted a number in the same order of magnitude and labeled it confirmed. Trivial-transcription test (Part 3.1b) applies — the precession rate is undergraduate-textbook physics with no obscurity carve-out.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Dome cites tau/I formula but no script or document shows intermediate steps connecting disc_radius / firmament_height / aetheric parameters to 4.87e-12. The formula reference is nominal.

holes_found.description: DUPLICATED-SENTENCES EDITORIAL BUG. The detail_evidence text contains two near-identical paragraphs back-to-back. Verbatim from current wins.json: '...the prediction fails on value even after the unit correction is applied. The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include gyroscopic precession in its 39-domain automated audit engine, confirming that even the model's author does not treat this as a computationally validated prediction. Even correcting the units charitably from rad/s² to rad/s, the dome's value 4.87×10⁻¹² falls 37% below Earth's measured precession angular velocity of 7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s — the prediction fails on value even after the unit correction is applied. The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include gyroscopic precession in its multi-domain automated audit engine, confirming that even the model's author does not treat this as a computationally validated prediction.' Both the 'Even correcting...' sentence and the 'monitor.py does not include...' sentence appear twice. Differences: the second monitor.py sentence reads 'multi-domain' instead of '39-domain' (likely a partial application of c2 minor recommendation #2, which suggested softening the hardcoded count). What appears to have happened: an editor added the softened version intending to REPLACE the original but instead APPENDED it, leaving both copies in the rendered prose. This is reader-facing and visible to anyone expanding the WIN-021 evidence panel — it damages credibility because it makes our review look like it was machine-pasted without proofreading. The 75% evidence-length growth flagged by change detection is entirely this duplication, not net-new content.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the duplicate. Keep ONE copy of each sentence. Recommended kept text: keep the 'multi-domain' phrasing (consistent with c2's minor recommendation about not hardcoding the domain count) and drop the '39-domain' copy of the same sentence. Likewise drop the duplicate 'Even correcting the units charitably...' sentence — keep only one occurrence.

holes_found.description: IAU citation URL still points to https://www.iau.org/ (the IAU homepage), not to the IAU 2006 Resolution B1 or to Capitaine et al. 2003 A&A 412, 567-586. This was flagged in the c2 'citations_unchecked' list with the same recommendation — it has not been addressed. The link works but doesn't directly substantiate the 50.2881 arcsec/yr figure.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace https://www.iau.org/ with a deeper link, e.g., https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf or a DOI for Capitaine et al. 2003 (A&A 412, 567).

holes_found.description: Defender's escape hatch left open. A dome defender can still argue: 'The 4.87e-12 rad/s² is the angular ACCELERATION of precession in the aetheric reference frame, not Earth's instantaneous angular velocity — you're comparing different physical quantities.' Our text shows the units are dimensionally inconsistent with the label 'precession rate' but does NOT explicitly head off the 'we meant acceleration on purpose' rebuttal. The c2 review's stronger_arguments section noted this defense (VLBI shows precession rate is near-constant, so a specific acceleration is unfalsifiable at 10⁻¹² scale) but the text was never updated to encode that point. As a result, a hostile reader can credibly claim we strawmanned by treating the rad/s² as an error when (they will say) it was the deliberate quantity.
holes_found.recommendation: Add ~1 sentence to detail_evidence preempting the 'we meant acceleration' defense, e.g.: 'A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional (predicting precession ACCELERATION, not rate) — but VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales, so a specific predicted angular acceleration at 10⁻¹² rad/s² is below any observational threshold and therefore unfalsifiable. The dome cannot have it both ways: either the units are wrong, or the prediction is unobservable.'

stronger_arguments.description: The post-hoc paragraph in detail_verdict_text now invokes the 'trivial-transcription test' (Part 3.1b) — that's a real strengthening since c2. It shifts the argument from 'this number is wrong' to 'this number can't have been predicted in any meaningful sense, even if it had been right.' That is a stronger structural argument because it doesn't depend on whether the dome's value is 37% off or 0% off — the post-hoc structural problem applies regardless. Recommend keeping this paragraph and considering whether to elevate it (it currently lives in the verdict text, but the structural argument arguably belongs at the top of the evidence text where readers see it first).
sources: Capitaine et al. 2003, A&A 412, 567 (the actual IAU 2006 source)

kernel_of_truth.description: Earth genuinely is a precessing gyroscope (data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_evidence acknowledges this in the 'luni-solar precession rate is well-established at 50.2881 arcsec/yr' line). The tau/I framework the dome cites IS classical mechanics for a torque-driven spinning body — that part of the dome's physical intuition is correct. The order of magnitude (~10⁻¹² in SI angular units) is in the right neighborhood. Identifying precession as a fundamental observable worthy of prediction is sensible.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being conceptually in-bounds doesn't save WIN-021 because the prediction (data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_evidence) makes three independent failures: wrong units (rad/s² is angular acceleration, not the angular velocity a precession rate requires), wrong number (37% below the IAU 2006 value of 7.7257e-12 rad/s computed from 50.2881 arcsec/yr), and no derivation (raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 presents the value statically with no calculation path). The post-hoc structural critique in data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_verdict_text further removes the only remaining defense — that the value was somehow predicted — by noting it's textbook physics and the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call. The classical-mechanics kernel of correctness lives upstream of the actual claim; the actual claim fails on every dimension that matters for a confirmation.

cycle_comparison.changes_since_cycle_2: c2 had 1 major + 2 minor holes. Major (monitoring tag wrong, hardcoded → none): FIXED in wins.json (current code_analysis.monitoring is 'none'). Minor #1 (39-domain hardcoded count): FIXED but BUG-INTRODUCED — the softened 'multi-domain' version was appended to the text instead of replacing the '39-domain' copy, leaving both. Minor #2 (finding too brief): FIXED — finding now reads 'Units error (rad/s² vs rad/s) and 37% value mismatch; no derivation connects dome geometry to the claimed number' (currently 112 chars vs 105 in c2 fingerprint, matches the c2 recommended phrasing). New content also added: the post-hoc structural critique paragraph in detail_verdict_text invoking Part 3.1b.
cycle_comparison.new_issues_found: 1 major (the duplicate-sentence editorial bug), 1 moderate (defender's-rebuttal preemption still missing), 1 minor (IAU URL still generic homepage from c2 unchecked-citations).
cycle_comparison.remaining_from_cycle_2: IAU URL link upgrade — c2 noted as 'citations_unchecked,' still unaddressed.

deeper_analysis:
The change-detection scan flagged WIN-021 because evid_pct grew 75% (1085→1900) and vtxt_pct grew >5x (189→1074) since the 2026-04-07 c2 review. Investigating the deltas: the verdict-text growth IS new content (the post-hoc structural critique paragraph), and that addition strengthens our argument. The evidence-text growth, however, turns out to be entirely the duplicated-sentences bug. There is no new evidence content; instead, an attempted softening edit (replacing '39-domain' with 'multi-domain' per c2 minor recommendation #2) was applied as an APPEND rather than a REPLACE, leaving both versions side-by-side. This is a pure editorial defect, not a substantive review evolution. 

The verdict 'Misleading' continues to hold — the three-failure argument (units / value / circularity) is intact and no new defender has surfaced that would shift to 'Refuted by Data' or another category. The post-hoc structural addition in verdict text actually further reinforces 'Misleading' because it shows the prediction fails on multiple independent grounds (one of which doesn't even depend on whether the number is right). 

The one item I'd flag for forward attention: the c2 review's strongest_argument (about predicting precession acceleration being unfalsifiable at 10⁻¹² rad/s² per VLBI) was identified as a stronger angle but never made it into the rendered text. That strongman defense remains live for any dome defender who reads our text; we should either preempt it explicitly (HOLE-003 recommendation) or accept that we're carrying an open hole on the rhetorical surface.

citations_verified: IAU 2006 angular velocity — independently computed: 50.2881 arcsec/yr × π/648000 rad/arcsec / (365.25 × 86400 s/yr) = 7.7257e-12 rad/s; period 2π/ω = 25,772 yr. Matches dome reference.
37% discrepancy claim — independently computed: (7.7257 - 4.87)/7.7257 = 36.96%, rounds to 37%. Verified.
citations_unchecked: https://www.iau.org/ — generic homepage URL from c2 still in text. Resolves but doesn't directly substantiate the 50.2881 arcsec/yr figure. Recommended to upgrade to IAU 2006 Resolution B1 PDF or Capitaine et al. 2003 DOI.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has read our review's six methodological principles could mount the following defense: 'Your critique conflates two separable questions. (1) Is the value 4.87e-12 rad/s² physically interpretable? Yes — under the dome's tau/I framing, it is the angular acceleration produced by aetheric torque on the precessing system. The reviewer treats this as a units error because they assume the dome meant rad/s, but the dome explicitly cites tau/I, which is dimensionally rad/s². (2) Is the value falsifiable? Yes, in principle — VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure the precession rate to high precision over decades, and the second derivative of that measured rate (which is what an acceleration prediction would target) is observable in the long-term geodetic record. The 37% comparison the reviewer draws is between the dome's predicted ANGULAR ACCELERATION and Earth's ANGULAR VELOCITY — that is the reviewer's category error, not the dome's. The structural post-hoc argument also fails because the dome's predicted quantity (acceleration in aetheric reference frame) is not what's textbook — only the related velocity (50.2881 arcsec/yr) is undergraduate physics; predicting an acceleration in a non-inertial frame is not a trivial-transcription.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add ~2 sentences to detail_evidence between the units paragraph and the 'circularity' paragraph that close this escape hatch directly. Suggested text: 'A defender might rescue the units by claiming the dome predicts angular ACCELERATION (rad/s²) rather than rate (rad/s) — that the tau/I formula is dimensionally honest. But this rescue costs more than it saves: VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure the precession rate as constant to far better than 10⁻¹² rad/s² over multi-decade baselines, so a specific predicted angular acceleration at this scale is below the observational threshold and therefore unfalsifiable. The dome cannot have it both ways — either the units are wrong (the originally-stated reading), or the prediction is unobservable (the rescued reading). Both readings fail the test of being a confirmed prediction.' This is the c2 stronger_argument made textual; it preempts the strongest defender available and converts the rescue attempt into a worse failure (unfalsifiability).

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-021 sits in a cluster of 38 WINs with code_analysis (monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false). WIN-021 is the ONLY one in that cluster making a numerical claim in rad/s units (a precession rate). The post-hoc trivial-transcription argument we're now invoking in WIN-021's detail_verdict_text is fully consistent with the same argument applied to other monitoring=none WINs (e.g., WIN-001 Tesla 11.78 Hz Schumann resonance, WIN-050/051 tidal periods). Our cross-WIN treatment is internally consistent — we don't accidentally credit one transcribed textbook value while denying another. The IAU 2006 figure cited here (50.2881 arcsec/yr / 7.7257e-12 rad/s / 25,772 yr) is a well-established source not used elsewhere in the review, so no citation conflict.

claims_checked: IAU 2006 precession rate 50.2881 arcsec/yr → 7.73e-12 rad/s (we wrote 7.73e-12)
Period 25,772 years (we mention this implicitly via the 25,772-year cycle)
quantitative_verification.computations: node compute: 50.2881 × (Math.PI/648000) / (365.25 × 86400) = 7.7257e-12 rad/s. (7.7257 − 4.87) / 7.7257 = 0.3696 → 36.96%, rounds to 37%. Period = 2π / 7.7257e-12 / (365.25 × 86400) = 25,772 yr exactly. All three quantitative claims in our detail_evidence are verified.
